page: Amazon Web Services g5.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, the instance is equipped with four NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs, delivering a total of 89 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of 3800 GB of NVMe SSD, complemented by a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in large language model inference tasks. Prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models consistently rank in the top 10% of tested systems. The CPU demonstrates strong capabilities in cryptographic operations, scoring in the top tier for PassMark CPU encryption. Multi-core performance is average to strong, with PassMark CPU and physics tests scoring in the top 25%, while single-core performance in stress-ng is relatively poor, falling in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, and database operations like Redis SET show high throughput.

Given the dense integration of four NVIDIA GPUs, high-speed local NVMe storage, and 96 vCPUs, this instance offers a specialized hardware profile designed for resource-heavy workloads. It is highly suitable for deep learning, LLM inference, ray tracing, and image processing. The combination of high-capacity local NVMe storage and strong database benchmark results also makes it a viable option for data-intensive applications and high-throughput database operations that require GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services g5.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 7R32 vCPUs running at 3.3 GHz, 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It is equipped with four NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs providing a total of 89 GB of VRAM, supported by a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier capabilities in large language model inference, prompt processing, and cryptographic operations, alongside strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance. While single-core CPU performance is relatively low, the instance excels in parallelized tasks. This hardware profile is optimized for demanding workloads such as LLM deployment, ray tracing, image processing, and high-throughput database operations requiring GPU acceleration.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, four NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs with 89 GB VRAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5.24xlarge features 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, four NVIDIA A10G GPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD for GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: A high-density GPU instance featuring four NVIDIA A10G accelerators, local NVMe storage, and top-tier large language model inference performance.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs with 89 GB total VRAM for parallel acceleration
96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 system memory
3800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in LLM inference and prompt processing
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Best suited for deep learning, ray tracing, and GPU-accelerated databases
